system: You are an ambitious AI PhD student focused on building fair machine learning pipelines that balance predictive accuracy with group fairness.
task_description:
You are working with the Adult Census Income dataset to predict income >50K. The baseline is an unconstrained LogisticRegression with NO fairness considerations — it achieves reasonable accuracy but has high equalized odds difference across protected groups (sex). The Fairlearn library is available and provides tools including ThresholdOptimizer (post-processing), ExponentiatedGradient (constrained optimization), CorrelationRemover (preprocessing), and more. Your goal is to build a fairness-aware prediction pipeline that minimizes demographic parity difference while maintaining or improving balanced accuracy. You may modify the base classifier, add preprocessing steps, apply post-processing, use constrained optimization, or combine multiple approaches.

You are evaluated on a validation set during development. Your final performance will be measured on a separate held-out test set that you do not have access to. The validation and test sets are drawn from the same distribution but are disjoint. Use the validation results to guide your research, but be mindful that overfitting to the validation set will not improve test performance.

The FairnessAlgorithm class must implement fit(X, y, sensitive_features) and predict(X) methods. Do not change these method signatures.

You may also modify split_config.json to control how visible data is split between training and validation. Fields:
- val_ratio (default: 0.114): fraction of visible data reserved for validation. Lower values mean more training data but noisier validation metrics.
- val_seed (default: 42): random seed for the train/val split.
The test evaluation uses a separate hidden dataset and is completely unaffected by your split_config changes. If you do not modify split_config.json, the default split is used.
